characters.name: Amita Ramanujan
characters.description: Amita RamanujanPortrayed byNavi RawatFull NameAmita RamanujanBiographical InformationBornAmita RamanujanJune 10, 1975 at 06:31 AM.Cedars Sinai Hospital, Los Angeles, CaliforniaGenderFemaleOccupationStudent atCalSci(formerly)Professor atCalSciFBI ConsultantStatusAliveRelationshipsParentsTapti Ramanujan(mother)Sanjay Ramanujan(father)RelativesUnnamed grandmotherFemale married cousinVenei(female cousin)Don Eppes(brother-in-law)Alan Eppes(father-in-law)Margaret Eppes†(mother-in-law)SpouseCharlie Eppes(husband)Show InformationFirst AppearancePilotLast AppearanceCause and EffectDr.Amita Ramanujan, Ph.D, Ph.D, (Navi Rawat) is a main character inNumb3rs. She begins the series asCharlie'sstudent where he is her thesis advisor and frequent collaborator. Then she becomes his girlfriend, later she becomes his fiancee, before she finally becomes his wife in the series finale. Amita Ramanujan was born on June 10th, 1975 at Cedars Sinai Hospital in Los Angeles at 06:31 AM. Her birth certificate was not witnessed and dated until June 12th, 1975 when a name other than Jane Doe Ramanujan was entered into the public record.[1] She is the daughter ofTapti Ramanujan(Gita Reddy) andSanjay Ramanujan(Brian George), Indian-American citizens who work for the Indian Ministry of Finance. At some point pre-series they moved back to India. Amita is a phenomenally intelligent, generally extrovert person with a similar voracious curiosity to that of Charlie andLarry. She is, in general, the voice of sanity and the real-world in their planned explorations of the outer reaches of thought. She has played an online alternate reality game calledPrimacysince she was an undergraduate. It's a temporary game which is part of an MMORPG, which goes on for years. Amita is part of an alliance who plays a few hours a week. Her avatar is Kali, the Goddess of Destruction. She chose the name/avatar when she was 19.

Bill Waldie appears in the episodeScorched.

characters.name: Brett Chandler
characters.description: Brett Chandlerheld a terrible secret about the death of his brother, Brian: he was accidentally shot when he and three other guys, Logan Oliver,Mark Green, and Josh Ryan, were all messing around with a gun. The guys swore to Brett they would never tell, but under blackmail, that they ride along with him in making it big in California's entertainment industry. When callgirl Tracy Meade, a doppelganger for young starlet Andrea Brown, drowned to death and was found at their mansion, it all still came to a head even though she was left in a canyon when the FBI received an anonymous tape revealing her death. A very much alive Andrea Brown was found out to be pirating movies, using Tracy's murder as blackmail. But when Logan was murdered, Brett was devastated and eventually revealed what happened to his brother out of grief over him and his now dead friend. His grief turned to rage once he realized Mark was the one who killed Logan and Tracy, taking a shot from Mark as Mark tried to flee unsuccessfully. Mark was shot own in a standoff, with Brett's fate left at either prison or not making it.

characters.name: Buck Winters
characters.description: When first seen, Buck Winters is a teenager caught up in the tempest that isCrystal Hoyle's life, a boy toy who wants to prove himself worthy of the older woman's love. He joins her on a whirlwind of death and debauchery, and when captured by the FBI tries to confess to all their crimes alone, looking to protect her. Crystal, however, has other plans, and kidnapsMegan Reevesin hopes of forcing the FBI to release her lover, and when she herself is gunned down trying to take outDon'steam, Buck ends up looking at life behind bars. A couple of years later he escapes, and hunts down Don, supposedly for revenge, but hoping, in fact, that he can push Don into doing the same thing he did to Crystal - put a bullet in Buck's head. Whether life really goes on for him or not thereafter is never revealed.

characters.name: Charlie Eppes
characters.description: Charlie EppesPortrayed byDavid KrumholtzFull NameCharles Edward EppesNicknamesCharlieChuckThe ProfessorBiographical InformationBornCharles Edward EppesSeptember 5, 1975 (Harvest)Los Angeles, CaliforniaGenderMaleOccupationHigh-level MathematicianProfessor at CalSciFBI consultantNSA consultantStatusAliveRelationshipsParentsAlan Eppes(father)Margaret Eppes†(mother)SiblingDon Eppes(brother)RelativesDoris(maternal great-aunt)Tommy(uncle)Irene(maternal great-aunt)Tapti Ramanujan(mother-in-law)Sanjay Ramanujan(father-in-law)PartnerSusan Berry(ex-girlfriend)SpouseAmita Ramanujan(wife)Show InformationFirst AppearancePilotLast AppearanceCause and EffectEpisode Count118 Some people drink, some gamble, I analyze data. Charles Edward “Charlie” Eppesis one of the two central characters on the showNumb3rs. Charlie is a mathematical genius and works as a professor of Applied Mathematics atCalifornia Institute of Science (CalSci), and as a math consultant with various government agencies such as the FBI and the NSA. In particular, he regularly helps his brother Don and Don's Violent Crimes team by using his mathematical skills. He will often recruit the help of his then-thesis student and later girlfriend/wife,Amita Ramanujan, and his mentor,Larry Fleinhardt. Charlie is stubborn and hardworking to the point of being obsessive. Alan once states that although Charlie is very observant in certain areas, he sometimes completely misses other things. Charlie is easily captivated by a problem presented to him and will work tirelessly to solve it, often staying up long hours in his garage with his chalkboards full of equations. He is often very enthusiastic about his work and eager to share excessive explanations with anyone who will pause to listen. He talks fast and is a little disorganized; never standing still, gesturing with his hands and stacking papers everywhere. Charlie also has a very strong sense of right versus wrong and feeling of guilt. When taking a polygraph test, he is extremely nervous and anxious, confessing to childhood crimes such as stealing a baseball card from a friend and accidentally killing his brother's class gerbil. Charlie is quite idealistic when it comes to social problems and relationships as evidenced clearly throughout the series, especially in season 5, when he questions his work with the FBI. Charlie can be incredibly stubborn and child-like when upset and holds strong grudges against people who he feels have wronged him. Charlie is described by Larry as egotistical, but he is extremely loyal to his family and friends, doing anything in his power to keep them healthy, safe, and happy. Charlie is occasionally a little naive when it comes to human behavior, being a child prodigy in the math world. However, this is not one of Charlie's prominent characteristics, as he is quite capable of handling himself properly in social situations. He does not have the same gift for language, and especially spelling, that he does for math, presumably

characters.name: Crystal Hoyle
characters.description: Crystal HoylePortrayed by Kim DickensNicknameCrys/ChrisBiographical InformationGenderFemaleShow InformationFirst Appearance"Spree"Last Appearance"Two Daughters"Crystal Hoyleis the driving force behind "Spree" and "Two Daughters", whilst her influence reaches out to shape "Arrow of Time", through her lover and accompliceBuck Winters. Crystal enthralls the teenager, and sets him off on a rampage of violence and excess, looking to prove some point that is never entirely clarified, seemingly even to herself. She has momentary regrets about her own unfitness as a mother, but in the end elects to drive straight at an FBI roadblock in a car crammed with grenades, looking for the "blaze of glory". Don Eppesshoots her dead with a sniper rifle at range, to protect his own people, and possibly in part as retribution for her abduction ofMegan Reeves. This comes back to haunt him when Buck tries to essentially commit "death by cop", looking to provoke Don into putting him out of his endless misery. Don seems to desire to help the younger man, even while sending him back to prison. Crystal's story moves Megan as well, since the FBI agent finds the former teacher a "road not taken" reflection of what her own life might have been.

characters.description: David SinclairPortrayed byAlimi BallardFull NameDavid SinclairBiographical InformationBornDavid SinclairBronx, New York CityGenderMaleOccupationFBI AgentStatusAliveRelationshipsSiblingsLinda (sister)Unnamed brother-in-lawShow InformationFirst AppearancePilotLast AppearanceCause and EffectEpisode Count114 [Talking toColby Granger] You, my friend, are a prisoner of high standards and low social skills. David Sinclairis an FBI Agent inDon Eppes's unit, and the only other one to appear in all six seasons ofNumb3rs.

characters.name: Elaine Tillman
characters.description: Elaine Tillmanis a mother on a mission after finding out to her horror her husband was sexually victimizing their daughter. She was delighted to put an end to his horrific crimes by torturing him to death in a home for sale, but she didn't want to stop there. Going on online chat forums where pedophiles and predators were being hunting to be caught in the act and legally brought to justice, she found one such activist's son, Matt McClary, discussing hypothetical vigilantism against said felons. Elaine saw this as an opportunity for a calling she could live up to, thus with violent modifications and arrangements to cover tracks, she starting tracking and torturing to death more predators, leaving them at the scenes in houses for sale, making them record confessions, even covering her tracks by rerouting her Internet connections. She killed three more men with such records in such a way, but once Matt was eventually found, so was she. She was caught torturing another would-be victim, butMegan Reevesappealed to her, reminded her of her conscience, reassuring her the predator she was killing would be fully prosecuted with the evidence acquired because of her. With that, she surrendered to her arrest, watching as her would-be victim was taken into custody as well.

characters.name: Ian Edgerton
characters.description: Ian EdgertonPortrayed byLou Diamond PhillipsBiographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationFBI AgentShow InformationFirst AppearanceSniper ZeroLast AppearanceCause and EffectEpisode Count9 "Colonel in Special Ops said he was the bastard son of Clint Eastwood and Yoda." Ian Edgerton(Lou Diamond Phillips) is an FBI Agent, and probably the most memorable character onNumb3rsrelative to his total of appearances (9). He is not part of the LA team led byDon Eppesbut rather a maverick, a hunter who brings in the criminals no other agent can seem to track down. He first appears in "Sniper Zero", setting his real-world experience againstCharlie'smodels. However, from the beginning he shows respect for Charlie and the other academics which is generally reciprocated, and seems (for him) genuinely upset in "Cause and Effect" that he didn't get an invitation to the lucky professor's wedding toAmitaas the curtain comes down. Edgerton is there for a large number of pivotal moments in the tale of the Eppes boys, from Don shootingCrystal Hoyleto the rescue of Amita from her abductors. InUltimatum, he's falsely accused of corruption and mistrusted by his fellow agents, but clears his own name by the end of the episode, restoring the team's faith in him.

characters.name: Ignacio Nadal
characters.description: Ignacio Nadalis a college student deep into a card counting scam with his two friends, taught by a disgraced professor who knew how to beat the casinos and hired by a bouncer to launder much of their illicit profits. When Ignacio got greedy, he killed the others involved in the scam to keep the profits for himself. He nearly murdered the professor as well, but he was tackled by the FBI and arrested, bragging he did it all for the money he wanted to go and take for himself that was right in front of him.

characters.name: John Buckley
characters.description: John Buckley(Fisher Stevens) is a highly intelligent, charismatic and manipulative mass kidnapper/con artist, who almost pulls off a fake abduction for millions of dollars in "Jacked". He is imprisoned, but offers his services toDon Eppesand his team in the matter of the "Con Job", assistance which he is able to parlay into his own freedom once more. He is last seen aprés-ski in Europe, offering to cross swords, or clink glasses, with Don at some later date, a third round which regrettably never takes place. Buckley is an excellent example of the 'bait-and-switch' which the writers so often used when first introducing a character. He reappeared, but only once.

characters.name: Kim Hall
characters.description: Kim HallPortrayed by Sarah Wayne CalliesBiographical InformationBornKimberly HallGenderFemaleOccupationSecret ServiceFBI Agent (formerly)StatusAliveRelationshipsParentsUnnamed parentsPartnerDon Eppes(ex-fiancé)Show InformationOnly AppearanceCounterfeit RealityKim Hallis a Secret Service Agent working with counterfeit money. She was previously an FBI Federal Agent working withDon Eppesat the Albuquerque, New Mexico office. Don Eppesand Kim Hall while they were a couple in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Kim Hall worked as an FBI Agent in Albuquerque, New Mexico. This is presumably where she met Don Eppes. The relationship was serious enough that they lived together and were engaged, but it ended when Don's mother got cancer and he moved back to Los Angeles to be closer to her. The breakup happened about three years before "Counterfeit Reality". She sent the engagement ring back to him in the mail.

The breakup happened about three years before "Counterfeit Reality". She sent the engagement ring back to him in the mail.

characters.name: Larry Fleinhardt
characters.description: Larry FleinhardtPortrayed byPeter MacNicolFull NameLawrence FleinhardtNicknameLarryBiographical InformationBornLawrence FleinhardtGenderMaleOccupationPhysicistFBI consultantProfessor(formerly)StatusAliveRelationshipsParentsUnnamed mother †Unnamed father †RelativesLouise(aunt)PartnersMegan Reeves(ex-girlfriend)Sandra Darwinkle(ex-girlfriend)Show InformationFirst AppearancePilotLast AppearanceCause and EffectEpisode Count93 Unnamed father † Within the small sphere of our lives we can stare into the past, but all we can really change is our future. Larry is a complex man, crammed with quirks, such as his predilection for white food, but often serves as the counterweight toDon'spragmatism, reminding Charlie of the need to broaden his scope of inquiry, and look beyond the immediate, and obviously necessitous. Larry spends the time from the end ofKiller ChattoThe Art of Reckoningticking a big item off his bucket list - in space.

characters.name: Margaret Eppes
characters.description: Margaret EppesPortrayed by JoBeth WilliamsFull NameMargaret EppesBiographical InformationBornMargaret MannSeptember 20Died2004Los Angeles, CaliforniaGenderFemaleOccupationLawyerComposer/PianistStay-At-Home-Mother/WifeStatusDeceasedRelationshipsSiblingsDoris(sister or sister-in-law)Tommy(brother or brother-in-law)ChildrenDon Eppes(oldest son)Charlie Eppes(youngest son)RelativesIrene(aunt)Robin Brooks(oldest son's fiancée)Amita Ramanujan(daughter-in-law)Tapti Ramanujan(youngest son's mother-in-law)Sanjay Ramanujan(youngest son's father-in-law)SpouseAlan EppesShow InformationOnly AppearanceHot ShotEpisode Count1 [To Alan] I just want you all to be happy. Although seen in motion only once ("Hot Shot"), Margaret,Alan'sbeloved wife andDonandCharlie'smuch-missed mom, is a constant presence throughout the show, often invoked by her surviving partner when the boys manage to set aside their differences. Her photos are scattered around the Eppes residence, and mementos abound. Margaret enjoyed jigsaw puzzles. She was also very organized, and a bit of a pack rat.
characters.personality_section: Margaret enjoyed jigsaw puzzles. She was also very organized, and a bit of a pack rat.

characters.name: Mark Green
characters.description: Mark GreenshadowedBrett Chandlerfor his life in the fast lane, but at a cost: he'd never tell what Brett, himself, and two of their friends were responsible for, the manslaughter of Brett's brother Brian when they messed around with a gun. But when Mark drowned in a bathtub Tracy Meade, callgirl doppelganger for movie-pirating starlet Andrea Brown, all the secrets were coming forward. The piracy operation became the least of his problems when Logan called the FBI wanting to tell the truth about Brian, leading to mark finding and shooting him to shut him up. Brett had nothing to lose at that point and came clean about Brian himself; Mark had nothing to lose either, so when Brett accused him angrily, he fired a shot at Brett and took a hostage over the pool. It obviously failed to work, asLiz Warner, a new agent on the team, appeared from his side and shot him into the pool, ending his deadly blackmail for good.

characters.name: Marshall Penfield
characters.description: Marshall PenfieldPortrayed byColin HanksBiographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationProfessor in MathematicsStatusAlive Marshall Penfield(Colin Hanks) has built much of a successful mathematical career around dissecting the Eppes Convergence, looking to prove it is flawed. He comes toCalScito lecture on the crack in its foundation which he has uncovered, pushing Charlie to reconsider his work, and strive to improve it. He went on at least one date with Dr.Amita Ramanujan, which prompted Dr.Charlie Eppesto finally make a move and ask her out.

characters.name: Marshall Penfield
characters.description: Marshall PenfieldPortrayed byColin HanksBiographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationProfessor in MathematicsStatusAlive Marshall Penfield(Colin Hanks) has built much of a successful mathematical career around dissecting the Eppes Convergence, looking to prove it is flawed. characters.name: Mason Lancer
characters.description: Mason Lanceris a U.S. Department of Justice prosecutor and a spy for the Chinese government. Lancer was born in Beijing to American parents and was eventually recruited as a mole.Colby Grangerwas briefly framed for the espionage, but his liaison, Michael Kirkland, assisted his prison escape to clear his name shortly before Kirkland was murdered. Lancer drugged and tortured Granger for info on the investigation, and in a standoff, Lancer and his accomplice,Dwayne Carter, were both shot dead. Granger's life was narrowly saved from an injection Lancer tried to kill him with.

characters.name: Matt Li
characters.description: Matthew "Matt" LiPortrayed by Matthew Yang KingFull NameMatthew LiNicknameMattBiographical InformationBornMatthew LiGenderMaleOccupationFBI TechStatusAliveShow InformationFirst AppearanceUncertainty PrincipleLast AppearanceCause and Effect Matt Li(Matthew Yang King) was an FBI Tech at the L.A. HQ who first appeared in "Uncertainty Principle", but didn't get a name until Season Four's "Velocity". He did, however, stick around for the whole run of the series, and was last seen in "Cause and Effect".

characters.name: Megan Reeves
characters.description: Megan ReevesPortrayed byDiane FarrFull NameMegan ReevesBiographical InformationBornMegan ReevesGenderFemaleOccupationProfiler/FBI Agent(formerly)StatusAliveRelationshipsParentsUnnamed parentsSiblings3 unnamed older sisters[1]RelativesUnnamed nephewPartnerLarry Fleinhardt(ex-boyfriend)Show InformationFirst AppearanceJudgment CallLast AppearanceWhen Worlds Collide Well, I didn't run away from home at 16, but I didn't walk either. And unlike Crystal, I can live with all of my decisions. I don't even regret most of them. Megan Reeves, likeTerry Lakebefore her, provides crucial insight into the inner workings of the criminal mind, and those of witnesses, and helps to develop frequent non-violent solutions to problems. She is in a relationship with Dr.Larry Fleinhardtfor some time, although when she decides the FBI is no longer for her, their relationship/friendship becomes long-distance. UnlikeNikki BetancourtandLiz Warner, Megan tends not to take an "anything you can do, I can do better" approach with the boys, and it is that level of trust, particularly when it is betrayed byColby's revelation as a spy, that leads her to conclude she's not cut out for this life. In contrast to Terry, Megan is often fondly recalled after she leaves, although even for the finale she does not make it back. From the beginning, since she is neither overwhelmed by his intellect nor driven to compete with him, Megan shows a comprehensive appreciation ofCharlie's contribution, and is often the one to gently steer him back towards their focus, rather than making the exercise the intellectual wellspring of fascination it could be if he's allowed to go off on a tangent. Megan quietly leaves at the end ofWhen Worlds Collide. Afterward,LarrytellsCharlieandAmitathat she's moving to Washington, D.C. to get her doctorate, and that her intent is to counsel women in prison.

characters.name: Mildred Finch
characters.description: Mildred "Millie" FinchPortrayed byKathy NajimyNicknameMillieBiographical InformationGenderFemaleOccupationPhysics/Math/Astronomy Division Chair at the California Institute of Science (CalSci)StatusAlive Mildred Finch(Kathy Najimy) bursts intoCharlie Eppes' world in Season 3's "Waste Not" as the "Physics/Math/Astronomy division chair" - in other words, his boss from pretty much any angle. She wants more of Charlie's time to be put into "collaborative projects", limiting his opportunities to help his brother. As she simultaneously boostsAmita'scareer, Charlie is left uncertain of how he feels about her. His confusion only increases when "Millie" takes a shine to his visitingdad, and vice versa, particularly since Alan does not, in general, date. She steamrollers "the Sean Connery of the Math Department", as she dubs Charlie, and is similarly a whirling dervish through the existence ofLarry Fleinhardt, evicting him from his steam tunnel, but working hard to reintegrate him with the community at large. She appears in another eight episodes ofSeason 3, slowly insinuating herself into the lives of all three of "the Eppes ladies" as she refers to them when looking to get competition for a poker game. As Millie observes of Charlie, "you've made my life a little more difficult, and a lot more interesting", a backhanded compliment he is happy to return.

characters.name: Nadine Hodges
characters.description: Nadine HodgesPortrayed bySarah CarterBiographical InformationGenderFemaleOccupationAssistant U.S. AttorneyStatusAlive Nadine Hodgesis an attorney in the DA's office with a considerable history withDon Eppes. She resurfaces in his life inJudgment Callprosecuting a strong suspect in the murder of a federal judge's wife, although for an earlier crime, and in front of said judge.

characters.name: Nikki Betancourt
characters.description: Nikki BetancourtPortrayed bySophina BrownBiographical InformationGenderFemaleOccupationFBI AgentLAPD(formerly)StatusAlive Nikki Betancourtjoins the Violent Crimes Unit shortly before season five'sHigh Exposureand proceeds to quickly clash with most of the team. However over the course of the show she warms up to them and she becomes a proper member of the team. Nikki spent four years in the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) before presumably attending Quantico to become an FBI Agent. She was then assigned to the Violent Crimes Unit in LA led byDon Eppes, where she replacedMegan Reeveson the team. She is a confident and assured woman who knows what she wants, and has very strong opinions about right and wrong,
characters.personality_section: She is a confident and assured woman who knows what she wants, and has very strong opinions about right and wrong,

characters.name: Nikki Betancourt

characters.name: Oswald Kittner
characters.description: Oswald KittnerPortrayed by Jay BaruchelFull NameOswald KittnerBiographical InformationBornOswald KittnerGenderMaleOccupationStudent at CalSciStatusAliveShow InformationFirst AppearanceHardballLast AppearanceDemocracy Oswald Kittner(Jay Baruchel) is a young, gifted prodigy who has crossed paths with the FBI a couple of times during their cases, starting when his equations were leaked and involved in a murder case, which ultimately put him in danger.

characters.name: Otto Bahnhoff
characters.description: Otto BahnoffPortrayed by John CarianiNicknameOttobahn (Autobahn)Biographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationEngineer(plasma physics and technology)FBI ConsultantStatusAlive Otto Bahnoff(John Cariani), who prefers to be called "Ottobahn" like Autobahn, is a brilliant but nervous and excitable CalSci engineer. Otto specializes in plasma physics and technology. Very little is known about Bahnoff's background and family. Although he attended a college or university where he gained at least one Ph.D before he was employed at theCalifornia Institute of Science (CalSci). Otto is a brilliant engineer specializing in plasma physics and technology, but he easily gets nervous and is very excitable. He speaks very fast, is constantly in motion, and is able to come to a conclusion just by glancing at a spec sheet he has never seen before along with some of Charlie's equations.
characters.personality_section: Otto is a brilliant engineer specializing in plasma physics and technology, but he easily gets nervous and is very excitable. He speaks very fast, is constantly in motion, and is able to come to a conclusion just by glancing at a spec sheet he has never seen before along with some of Charlie's equations.

characters.name: Robin Brooks
characters.description: Robin BrooksPortrayed by Michelle NoldenBiographical InformationGenderFemaleOccupationAssistant U.S. AttorneyStatusAliveRobin Brooks(Michelle Nolden) is an Assistant U.S. Attorney who works primarily out of L.A. and often collaborates with AgentDon Eppesand his team. This is rather convenient, considering that, for most of the time she's seen onscreen, the two of them are in a relationship. In the season 6 (and series) finale she accepts Don's proposal and becomes his fiancée. Robin comes with considerable baggage of her own, and she and Don prove that "the course of true love never did run smooth". There is equally no doubt, however, that she brings out parts of Don that would otherwise remain sequestered, such as his love ofSullivan's Travels.

characters.name: Robin Brooks
characters.description: Robin BrooksPortrayed by Michelle NoldenBiographical InformationGenderFemaleOccupationAssistant U.S. AttorneyStatusAliveRobin Brooks(Michelle Nolden) is an Assistant U.S. Attorney who works primarily out of L.A. and often collaborates with AgentDon Eppesand his team. characters.name: Roger Bloom
characters.description: Roger BloomPortrayed byHenry WinklerBiographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationFBI Agent(formerly)StatusAlive Roger Bloom(Henry Winkler) first appears in "Jack of All Trades" as an FBI Agent whose concern is what he perceives as justice, rather than the law. His actions basically get him cashiered out of the Bureau, so when the team, specificallyColbyandNikki, come knocking again ("Greatest Hits"), he's less than welcoming. This time, however, things go rather better all around, so when the D.B. Cooper heist is revisited ("Old Soldiers"), Colby manages to talk Roger into providing his insight. This time, though, the conflict is on the Eppes' home front, sinceAlanwas ardently anti-deployment in Vietnam, and Roger is a veteran. The older generation, however, eventually find a "live and let live" groove, tinged with mutual respect. Roger develops a particularly close relationship with Colby, and given the nature of Bureau work this may be beneficial to both.

characters.name: Roland Haldane
characters.description: Roland Haldane, the first major criminal in the show'sPilot, had the façade of a catering business, blowtorch and knife as part of the smoke screen. But Haldane was always a severely brutal serial rapist the FBI has been tracking, the blowtorch used to heat the symbol on the bottom of the knife, which he then used as a brand on 12 women he would rape over the course of months. The case became worse when he started to murder the women he raped, killing two women in a much shorter time span, as well as going back and doing the same to the last woman he raped before he started killing, Karen Silber.Don Eppeswas at a loss when leading the team, butCharlie Eppes, his brother, helped him in what would be his first consulting case by tracking the activity based on where the killer lived. That proved to fail after Karen Silber's death, which made Charlie consider an alternative: the rapist/murderer's workplace. Which proved to lead right to Haldane. Haldane, when confronted with the evidence and realizing being made, tried to takeDavid Sinclairas a hostage, but Don jumped on a distraction that made Haldane slip enough for Don to shoot him down and save David. Don told Charlie exactly what changed in the equation: his workplace never changed, but he moved from his home three weeks prior to the equation's input.
